title: packera × memmingeri
intro: Packera × memmingeri, also known as Memminger's ragwort, is a hybrid plant species within the Asteraceae family, resulting from the cross between Packera obovata and Packera serotina. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits associated with Packera × memmingeri include reduced inflammation and relief from gastrointestinal issues, as well as potential antibacterial and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is sometimes used externally as a poultice or salve to aid in wound healing.
